evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: enhance data quality, observability, and documentation; engineering best practices; accuracy and performance; promote reuse
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Repeated emphasis on quality, observability, documentation, and testing indicates rigour orientation.
evidence.domain_risk: SaaS analytics platform; no financial/regulatory signals
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Platform-focused analytics role without financial reporting or compliance language suggests moderate risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineering squads; implied product teams
evidence.data_team_maturity: 40+ data professionals; production dashboards; established tooling; multiple projects
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Large team, production infrastructure, and semantic modeling indicate mature data function.
evidence.jd_authorship: develop curated analytical datasets; semantic models; dimensional modeling; scalable data warehouse; self-serve analytics
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical language (semantic models, dimensional modeling, self-serve) demonstrates hiring manager perspective.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: data warehouse structures; self-serve analytics; internal data platform
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Focus on internal data infrastructure and analytics platform.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Role framed as supporting and improving platforms, not setting strategy.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: Standard analytics engineering work with no AI requirements.
evidence.testing_framing: apply engineering best practices including testing
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing mentioned as part of broader best practices without ownership responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability emphasized but not framed as risk-control measure.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Scaling and optimization of existing infrastructure.
